source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/231031 보완/A-TCF-5-156 Test Reports/8. [A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW] EN 62220-1-1 Test report.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Test report for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors tested by EN 62220-1-1.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Technical documentation report documenting physical, operational, functional, exposure, safety, and test-result characteristics of the digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
keyPoints: The report covers Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices and includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW have the same performance and usage except for size.
The devices use a CsI screen on a substrate and convert x-ray energy to light, then to electrical signals and 16-bit digital image data.
The device is not for fluoroscopic use and is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

concepts.name: EN 62220-1-1 test report
concepts.evidence: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

concepts.name: Detective Quantum Efficiency
concepts.evidence: Provide an estimate of Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE) at low spatial and temporal frequency.

concepts.name: Modulation Transfer Function
concepts.evidence: Provide a plot of the modulation or contrast transfer function of the device.

concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW device uses a CsI screen on a substrate.

concepts.name: Static x-ray detector
concepts.evidence: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

concepts.name: DICOM output conversion
concepts.evidence: The saved raw data from A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW will be transferred to the end user’s S/W and then converted to a DICOM or Bitmap file.

evidence.claim: The document is EN 62220-1-1 Test Report No. A-TCF-5-116 Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-116 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The report revision date is Nov 18, 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report Rev. Date Nov 18, 2022

evidence.claim: The report applies to A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The applicable standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for digital X-ray imaging device detective quantum efficiency in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: 12-289 EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW have the same performance and usage except for size.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same expect for size. (A-TCF-5-127)

evidence.claim: A1717MCW has a 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm active pixel area and 3,036 x 3,040 active pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW Active pixel area size 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 3,036 x 3,040 = 9,229,440

evidence.claim: A1417MCW and F1417MCW each have a 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm active pixel area and 2,466 x 3,040 active pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: A1417MCW Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 2,466 x 3,040 = 7,496,640 ... F1417MCW Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 2,466 x 3,040 = 7,496,640

evidence.claim: The detector uses CsI scintillator and passive pixel TFT panel architecture.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator • CsI ... TFT Panel • Basically, Pixel Arrays • Pixel Architecture: Passive pixel
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Architecture

evidence.claim: The detector frame rate is 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Frame Rate • 1 fps
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Frame Rate

evidence.claim: The device generates a digital x-ray image in response to a trigger signal and does not initiate the X-ray generator or control patient exposure settings.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW generates a digital x-ray image in response to a trigger signal, but does not initiate an X-ray generator or control the x-ray exposure setting for a patient.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Operation

evidence.claim: The detector outputs raw image data via 1Gbps Ethernet or wireless to an access point.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector outputs raw image data to the workstation, via 1Gbps Ethernet or Wireless to Access Point.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Data Output

evidence.claim: Acquisition software receives and stores 16-bit digital image data.
evidence.evidenceText: The acquisition software in the workstation receives the 16-bit digital image data and processes it. It is stored in the hard disk in a 16-bit format.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Data Output

evidence.claim: The saved raw data can be converted to DICOM or Bitmap by end-user software.
evidence.evidenceText: The saved raw data from A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW will be transferred to the end user’s S/W and then converted to a DICOM or Bitmap file.

evidence.claim: The device is not for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not for fluoroscopic.

evidence.claim: The device uses a CsI screen on a substrate that converts x-ray energy directly to light.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW device uses a CsI screen on a substrate. This scintillator is commercially available and converts x-ray energy directly to light.

evidence.claim: The photodiode arrays convert light to electrical signals proportional to light intensity.
evidence.evidenceText: The photodiode arrays convert light to electrical signals proportional to the intensity of the light.

evidence.claim: Measured DQE values at 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp are 69%, 62%, 49%, and 31% for A1717MCW; 69%, 61%, 50%, and 34% for A1417MCW; and 71%, 64%, 51%, and 35% for F1417MCW.
Measured MTF values at 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp are 84%, 65%, 30%, and 13% for A1717MCW; 85%, 65%, 33%, and 17% for A1417MCW; and 86%, 66%, 34%, and 17% for F1417MCW.
No under-scanning is possible; the active area is in full use.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm(A1717MCW), 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm(A1417MCW/F1417MCW) and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The minimum time between exposures is 8 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after an x-ray exposure requires least 1 second with window 0.5 second. And to stabilize offset 7 seconds are needed. Thus, the minimum time between exposures is 8 seconds.

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: The report lists regulatory approvals by reference to a safety report and EMC reports.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW safety report: REP001824, EMC report: NK-22-E-0811, NK-22-E-0814

evidence.claim: Line chart test pattern exposure condition was 75kVp, 100mA, 10mAs, SID 100cm.
evidence.evidenceText: Exposure Condition - 75kVp, 100mA, 10mAs, SID: 100cm

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly identified in the source text, despite source metadata authoritySignal indicating FDA.
Source path references A-TCF-5-156, but the document text identifies Document No. and Report No. as A-TCF-5-116.
Some OCR/math text appears degraded, including formulas and DQE criteria entries such as '3.0 lp/mm (0 ≥ 31%)'.
The source text states F1417MCW quantum-noise section heading but provides no visible F1417MCW dark noise or quantum noise limit values.
